task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution correctly implements the trimmed mean calculation, but could be improved with more explicit edge cases handling.
trace: First, the function checks for an empty list and returns `None`. If the input is not empty, it sorts the numbers in ascending order. Since we need to drop exactly one minimum and one maximum value, we consider two cases depending on the size of the sorted list (n). For lists with less than three elements, we return the mean of all elements excluding the first and last ones. For larger lists, we return the mean of all elements except the very first and the second to the last.
